01  ·  Your Role

Your Role &
Responsibilities

As a Local Operator, you are the on-the-ground person keeping TULU units running at their best.

Your goal is to ensure high quality upkeep of TULU's unit/s — including inventory management, product repairs, and technical troubleshooting — to maintain smooth operations and meet our service standards.
🔧
Unit Upkeep

Maintenance, restocking, cleaning, testing products, checking camera and updating signage.

📦
Inventory & Backroom

Organise backroom, report supply needs, check expiry dates, keep the area tidy.

🔄
Repairs & Replacements

Report and replace faulty items through the return/warranty process.

🛠️
Troubleshooting

Try AI assistant first. If unresolved, escalate to Area Manager via Slack.

Reporting Structure
Operations Manager
Area Manager
Local Operator (You)
How to Show Up
🚀
Proactive
If you don't do it, no one will.
💬
Communicative
If we don't know, we can't help.
🔍
Detail-Oriented
A nurtured unit is a successful one.

02  ·  Service Quality

Service Quality

TULU Units are our product and the face of TULU to our members. It's important to keep it maintained to the highest standard to ensure an amazing experience for anyone who visits or wants to rent.

02  ·  Quality Standards

TULU Unit &
TULU Shop

TULU Unit — Rental Store
Example → Organized, lit, labelled, and clean.

✨ Attractive

  • Products neatly organized, upright, facing user
  • Supplies in matching bags/bottles per unit
  • All lights and LEDs on and white

👆 Easy to Use

  • Number stickers on multi-quantity items
  • Trash can near unit
  • Monitor on, showing TULU app QR code
  • Camera green light on

🧹 Clean

  • Doors and shelves wiped, no dust
  • No trash inside or around the unit
TULU Shop — Snacks & Supplies
Example → Full shelves, labels forward, no gaps.

Presentation

  • Labels always facing user
  • All items visible from one viewpoint
  • Each item in its own row
  • Remove damaged items immediately

No Gaps — Always Full

  • If out of stock, expand adjacent rows
  • Stock from back of row forward
  • Adjust dividers to eliminate gaps
02  ·  Quality Standards

TULU Ride &
Backroom

TULU Ride — Scooters
Example → Docked, charging (blue light), helmets and U-locks present.

Presentability

  • Clean debris from scooters
  • Wipe down scooters, accessories, dock

Functionality

  • Confirm all accessories present
  • Verify scooters charging and functional
  • Contact Area Manager for issues
Backroom
Example → Organized by category, shop vs rent separated, no empty boxes.
An organised backroom makes your visits easier and faster.
  • Check expiry dates — dispose of anything expiring or close to expiry
  • Open all deliveries and organise products in the correct areas
  • Discard all empty boxes and trash immediately
  • Keep shop items and rent supplies in clearly separate areas
  • Display items side by side for better stock visibility

03  ·  Platforms

Platforms
Overview

Three tools you'll use as a Local Operator. The next section goes deep on OpsBuddy — your most important one.

📱 OpsBuddy
YOUR PRIMARY VISIT TOOL

Built into the TULU App, OpsBuddy is where you complete and log every visit task — restocking, inventory checks, issue reporting, and submitting your visit summary. You will use it on every single visit.

⚠️ Always update the TULU app before each visit — new features are released regularly.
Slack
ALL TEAM COMMUNICATION
  • You will be introduced to your Area Manager via your dedicated channel
  • You will be added to a shared channel with other operators for general updates and common topics
  • Residents compliance notices are shared here — check regularly to track any open gaps that need your attention
Do not write in any other Slack channels unless specifically instructed.
Deel
PAYMENTS & CONTRACTS
  • Deel is where your contract is created and managed
  • Submit your hours after each visit
  • Make sure to submit all hours for the month by the 1st of the following month
⚠️ Payment is applied by the 5th–6th of the next month for the previous month. Late submissions will not be paid until the following cycle.
04  ·  OpsBuddy

OpsBuddy —
How It Works

OpsBuddy is built into the TULU app. Every visit is logged and completed through it.

Always update the TULU app before each visit — improvements are released regularly.
How to Access OpsBuddy
1
Open the TULU App on your phoneMake sure you're signed in to your account.
2
Tap the menu icon (top left corner)This opens the main navigation.
3
Scroll down — tap the purple OpsBuddy buttonThis launches your visit dashboard.
4
Tap "Go to Schedule" to see your assigned buildingsSelect the building you're servicing today.
5
Use "View Remotely" to preview the visitPreview deliveries, shop, rent, and checks before arriving. Actions are disabled until you start on-site.
Visit Sections in OpsBuddy
1
Check-In
Start visit & report all received packages
2
Shop
Restock & keep unit inventory correct per item
3
Rent
Check rental items & report OOS
4
Print
Printer status and supplies
5
Checks
General checks, backroom, accessories & photo submission
6
Summary
Submit visit & report all issues
Out of Service Items

Step 1 — Try AI Troubleshooting

Use the AI assistant in OpsBuddy to troubleshoot first. Many problems can be resolved on the spot.

Step 2 — Escalate via Slack

If unresolved, report to your Area Manager via Slack with a clear description of the issue.

OpsBuddy — Source of Truth
Make sure all information and issues are reflected in OpsBuddy.
OpsBuddy is the source of truth for the TULU team. Once something is reported, we can assist with next steps — order requests, troubleshooting support, or any other assistance needed.
✨ AI Assistant — Troubleshooting Feature
🤖 Real-Time Troubleshooting

The AI Assistant in OpsBuddy helps you troubleshoot any rental item in real time, directly on-site. Report as many details as possible and follow the step-by-step guidelines to resolve issues without waiting for team support.

If the AI assistant cannot resolve the issue, report it to your Area Manager via Slack with a full description.

📖 PLEASE GO THROUGH THE FULL OPSBUDDY GUIDE STEP BY STEP BEFORE YOUR FIRST VISIT

This is the most important part of your preparation.

→ Open OpsBuddy Manual

05  ·  Visit Protocol

Visit Protocol

Follow this exact sequence at every visit — no exceptions.

🤓 Prepare — Get Onsite
1
Request backroom key & greet the front deskPick up the key from building reception. A friendly hello goes a long way!
2
Check for deliveries at the front deskCollect any packages and move them to the backroom before heading to the unit.
💥 Action — Go to the Unit
3
Open OpsBuddy — report all received packages via Check-In sectionStart your visit in OpsBuddy and log all deliveries you collected from the front desk.
4
Review Shop section — move all backroom items to the unitMake sure inventory levels are correct so users see the right availability in the app.
5
Complete Rent section and Checks sectionCheck all rental items, report any Out of Service items, and complete all unit checks (camera, monitor, lights, cleanliness).
📌 Make sure to report all issues and update inventory levels for all items — this is critical for members using the app.
📋 Wrap Up — Finalize
6
Submit Summary in OpsBuddyComplete the Summary section to officially close your visit and flag any unresolved issues.
7
Return key & say goodbyeReturn the key to the front desk or agreed drop-off. Every visit builds the relationship.

06  ·  Payments

Getting Paid

How to submit your hours and make sure you get paid on time every month.

Submitting Hours on Deel
INVOICE CYCLE
Submit all hours by the 1st of the following month. Payment is processed by the 5th. Hours submitted late are paid the following month.
Building Name: # total visits
Description: General maintenance and restocked shop

If average visit time exceeds 2 hours, include the reason and get Area Manager approval. Multiple buildings = one line per building.

⚠️ Additional hours (beyond your confirmed schedule) must be agreed with your Area Manager in advance and submitted with a full description after the visit via Deel. Your confirmed hours will be outlined in your Ops Lead Welcome Email.

Send your results to your Operations Lead to request account setup:

Please enter your email address before sending.

🚀 Next Steps
1
Register on the TULU App
Download and create your account — needed for every visit.
App StoreGoogle Play
2
Email Your Operations Lead
Use the email buttons above to send results and request account setup.
Subject: New Operator Setup | [Location], [Your Name]
Hi [Anastasia / Yussef],

I just completed the Local Operator Training and I'm ready to start on [desired start date] at [building name].

Please create my OpsBuddy, Deel and Slack accounts. I will await your invitations via email.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Fill in the purple fields before sending.

3
Await Your Account Invitations
Once your Ops Lead receives your results, they'll create your OpsBuddy, Deel and Slack accounts and send invitations to your email.
⏳ No need to do anything else — your Ops Lead will reach out once accounts are ready.
4
You're All Set! 🎉
Once accounts are active and your Area Manager introduces themselves via Slack, you're ready for your first visit!
